beginner.question: What does flex-direction determine?
beginner.answer: flex-direction determines the direction of the main axis, which controls how flex items are placed in the container.

beginner.question: What are the four values of flex-direction?
beginner.answer: The four values are row, row-reverse, column, and column-reverse.

beginner.question: What does flex-direction: row do?
beginner.answer: Items are placed horizontally from left to right (or right to left in RTL).

beginner.question: What does flex-direction: row-reverse do?
beginner.answer: Items are placed horizontally in reverse order, from right to left (or left to right in RTL).

beginner.question: What does flex-direction: column do?
beginner.answer: Items are placed vertically from top to bottom.

beginner.question: What does flex-direction: column-reverse do?
beginner.answer: Items are placed vertically in reverse order, from bottom to top.

beginner.question: What is the default value of flex-direction?
Does flex-direction affect the order of items visually?
beginner.answer: Yes, it reverses the visual order but does not change the HTML source order.

beginner.question: How does flex-direction affect the main axis?
beginner.answer: flex-direction defines the main axis; row makes the main axis horizontal, column makes it vertical.

beginner.question: How does flex-direction affect the cross axis?
beginner.answer: The cross axis is always perpendicular to the main axis; row gives a vertical cross axis, column gives a horizontal cross axis.

intermediate.question: How does flex-direction: row-reverse affect justify-content?
intermediate.answer: justify-content: flex-start places items at the right edge (opposite of row) because the main axis starts from the right.

intermediate.question: How does changing flex-direction affect align-items?
intermediate.answer: align-items always works on the cross axis; in row it's vertical, in column it's horizontal.

intermediate.question: How do you create a column layout with Flexbox?
intermediate.answer: Set flex-direction: column on the flex container; items stack vertically.

intermediate.question: What happens to width properties in flex-direction: column?
intermediate.answer: Width is still respected as the cross-axis size (horizontal), while the main-axis size (vertical) can be controlled by height.

intermediate.question: How does flex-wrap interact with flex-direction: column?
intermediate.answer: flex-wrap: wrap with column direction causes items to wrap into new columns, not new rows.

intermediate.question: How does writing mode (vertical-rl) affect flex-direction: row?
intermediate.answer: In vertical writing mode, row follows the inline direction (vertical), changing the main axis to vertical.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between row-reverse and order property for reversing?
intermediate.answer: row-reverse reverses all items; the order property allows selective reordering of individual items.

intermediate.question: How does margin: auto behave differently in row vs column direction?
intermediate.answer: In row, margin: auto absorbs horizontal space; in column, it absorbs vertical space.

intermediate.question: How do percentage values in flex-basis work with column direction?
intermediate.answer: With column, flex-basis: 50% refers to 50% of the container's height (the main axis size).

intermediate.question: How does the gap property work in column direction?
intermediate.answer: gap applies vertical spacing between items in column mode (instead of horizontal in row mode).

advanced.question: How does the flex-direction property interact with CSS logical properties?
advanced.answer: flex-direction maps to logical directions: row maps to inline, column maps to block, adapting to writing mode.

advanced.question: How does flex-direction affect the interpretation of min-height in flex items?
advanced.answer: In column direction, min-height constrains the main-axis size; min-width constrains the cross-axis size.

advanced.question: What happens to absolutely positioned children when flex-direction changes?
advanced.answer: Absolutely positioned children are not flex items, but their offset parents include flex container, and direction affects positioning context.

advanced.question: How does flex-direction: column handle horizontal centering with margin: auto?
advanced.answer: margin-left: auto and margin-right: auto in column direction still center horizontally (cross-axis), not vertically.

advanced.question: How does flex-direction work with the align-content property?
advanced.answer: In row direction, align-content distributes rows vertically; in column direction, it distributes columns horizontally.

advanced.question: How does the multi-keyword flex property change meaning with flex-direction?
advanced.answer: flex: 1 always refers to the main-axis growth; in row it grows width, in column it grows height.

advanced.question: How do you create a horizontal scrolling container using flex-direction: row?
advanced.answer: Set overflow-x: auto; flex-wrap: nowrap; on the container, allowing horizontal scroll without wrapping.

advanced.question: How does the flex-flow shorthand simplify direction and wrap settings?
advanced.answer: flex-flow: column wrap sets both flex-direction: column and flex-wrap: wrap in one declaration.

advanced.question: How does changing flex-direction affect the order of tab navigation?
advanced.answer: Tab order follows source order, not visual order, so row-reverse does not affect keyboard tab flow.

advanced.question: How do you apply different flex-direction values at different breakpoints?
advanced.answer: Use media queries: .container { flex-direction: column; } @media (min-width: 768px) { .container { flex-direction: row; } }.
